headline: Shutdown Deal Loses Traction. Hope of Relief for Air Travelers, TSA Workers Is Dimming.
summary: Transportation Security Administration agents’ hopes of getting paid soon were dashed Wednesday as initial support for the latest Department of Homeland Security funding deal fizzled out.  Republican senators presented Democrats with their most promising deal yet on Tuesday to end the 38-day shutdown, which has left the security agents without pay.  The deal would fund all of DHS, which oversees TSA, except for the migrant-removal activities of Immigration and Customs Enforcement, another DHS agency.

raw.headline: Shutdown Deal Loses Traction. Hope of Relief for Air Travelers, TSA Workers Is Dimming.

The deal would fund all of DHS, which oversees TSA, except for the migrant-removal activities of Immigration and Customs Enforcement, another DHS agency.

headline: Trump's 15-Point Iran Plan Could Trigger A Snapback Rally In These 10 War-Battered Stocks
summary: The U.S. reportedly sent Iran a 15-point peace plan covering missiles, nuclear programs, and Hormuz maritime routes. Polymarket prices a 48% chance of a ceasefire by April 30. These 10 Russell 1000 stocks — down 17% to 33% since the war began — are first in line for the rebound.
